inputs.prompt: Organisation: Clearwater Analytics. What it does: Provides data analytics and reporting software for local government finance departments. Founded: 2011. Geography: Offices in Manchester and Edinburgh, serves 140 local authorities across the UK. Scale: 85 employees, processes £4.2 billion in public spending data annually. Website: clearwateranalytics.example.com.
Organisation: Greenleaf Design Studio. What it does: Graphic design for non-profit organisations. Founded: 2018. Location: Bristol, UK. Website: greenleafdesign.example.com.

Organisation: Nordic Health Initiative. What it does: Runs cross-border healthcare research programmes connecting hospitals and universities across Scandinavia. Founded: 2007. Geography: Headquarters in Copenhagen, partners in all five Nordic countries. Scale: 120 staff, has facilitated 340 research collaborations since founding, €28M in research funding distributed. Mission: Improving patient outcomes through collaborative Nordic health research. Website: nordichealthinitiative.example.org. Tone: Mission-driven but factual.

Organisation: Gulf Futures Forum. What it does: Convenes policymakers, researchers, and private sector leaders across six Gulf states to develop frameworks for economic diversification beyond hydrocarbons. Founded: 2015. Geography: Headquartered in Muscat, Oman, with programme offices in Riyadh and Abu Dhabi. Scale: 45 staff, has published 68 policy papers, convened 22 ministerial-level summits, and trained 1,200 civil servants in diversification planning. Mission: Accelerating the transition to sustainable, knowledge-based economies in the Gulf region. Website: gulffuturesforum.example.org. Tone: Authoritative and diplomatic.

Organisation: Brightpath Global. What it does: We do lots of things — consulting, community building, innovation, sustainability, digital transformation, thought leadership, and strategic partnerships. Founded: 2020. Location: Registered in London but mostly virtual. Scale: Between 5 and 12 people depending on how you count contractors. Website: brightpathglobal.example.com.

Organisation: Watershed Trust. What it does: Restores degraded watersheds in the Western Ghats of India by partnering with tribal communities and smallholder farmers to implement contour bunding, reforestation, and spring rejuvenation. Founded: 2012. Geography: Headquarters in Pune, field offices in three districts across Maharashtra and Karnataka. Scale: 38 full-time staff, 210 trained community watershed volunteers, has restored 14,500 hectares across 87 villages, and revived 42 seasonal springs to year-round flow. Mission: Securing rural water security through community-led landscape restoration. Website: watershedtrust.example.org. Tone: Grounded, evidence-led, accessible.

Organisation: Canopy Health. What it does: Currently provides AI-powered triage software for rural clinics in sub-Saharan Africa, routing patients to the nearest appropriate facility based on symptom analysis and real-time bed availability. Previously operated as a telemedicine video platform (2018-2021) before pivoting to triage logistics after discovering that connectivity gaps made video consultations unreliable. Founded: 2018 (original company), pivoted 2021. Geography: Headquarters in Nairobi, Kenya, with deployments in 140 clinics across Kenya, Uganda, and Rwanda. Scale: 52 employees, processes 8,000 triage decisions per month, has reduced unnecessary referral travel by an estimated 35% in partner clinics. Funding: $6.2M raised across seed and Series A. Website: canopyhealth.example.com. Tone: Startup-confident but honest about the pivot.
